fields.description:
Increase market share by making our devices easy to find, learn how to use, and integrate into the customer's life seamlessly.

 

 

It's worth noting that bone-anchored hearing aids carry a social stigma. We seek to overcome that through smart design and financial efficiency.
fields.name: 適合性

fields.description: Increase revenue from teenaged patients 13-17 by 64% by FY 2017.
fields.name: Teenagers 13-17

fields.description: Increase revenue from in adult patients 18-24 by 56% by FY 2017.
fields.name: Adults 18-24

fields.description: Increase revenue from among adult patients 25-34 by 38% by FY 2017.
fields.name: Adults 25-34

fields.description: Increase usage among adult patients 35+ by 18% by FY 2017.
fields.name: Adults 35+

fields.description: Increase market share in Europe by 20% by 2017.
fields.name: Europe

fields.description: Increased market share in Asia by 13% by 2017.
fields.name: Asia

fields.description: Increased market share in North America by 42% by 2017.
fields.name: North America

fields.description: Increase market share in South America by 64% by 2017.
fields.name: South America

fields.description: Time and time again, we hear that our customers want their device to integrate seamlessly with their lives. This includes the device itself, but also the technology surrounding it that may not even be directly a part of the device.
fields.name: Usability

fields.description: Off the shelf, our devices cost an average of $10,000. Through increased efficiency in our use of technology and better materials, drop the average off the shelf price by 50% by the end of the 2017 fiscal year.
fields.name: Low-Cost, High-Quality

fields.description:
The hearing aid should be more adoptable than other comparable hearing aids.  This will be validated by using a patient focus group.  A patient from the group will be given a set of tasks and the time to complete the tasks will be measured.

 

Each step in the validation is a separate patient.
fields.name: Intuitive Design - Battery Replacement

testCaseSteps.action: Give the user a packaged battery and measure the time it takes them to insert the battery into the device
Give a second user a packaged battery and have them watch a 1 minute video describing the battery replacement
Give the second user a packaged battery and measure the time it takes them to insert the battery and re-attach the device

fields.description: Part of adoptability is ease of use and intuitive design.  In this validation, a focus group of user will be asked if they can identify the meaning of the graphic labels on the device.  We expect 80% of group members to indicate the correct meaning within 5 seconds of viewing the graphic
fields.name: Intuitive Design - Common Labelling

testCaseSteps.action: Display "Battery Door" label to patient
Display "Volume Control" label to patient
Display "Mobile App Icon" label to patient

fields.description: Patients should be able to easily complete personal hygiene activities without interference from the hearing aid.  A focus group of patients will be used to conduct the validation.
fields.name: Ease of Personal Hygiene - Bathing

testCaseSteps.action: Distribute the hearing aid to 10 patients
Survey patients to determine any issues involved with bathing

fields.description: Patients should be able to wash their face
fields.name: Ease of Personal Hygiene - Face

testCaseSteps.action: Distribute hearing aid to 10 patients soon after surgery
Have the patients wash their faces with the hearing aid attached
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: Patients are able to wash in less than 1 minute with no reports of discomfort

testCaseSteps.action: Have the patients wash their faces with the hearing aid unattached
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: Patients are able to wash in less than 1 minute with no reports of discomfort

fields.description: The hearing aid should have manual controls that are of a sufficient size and location to allow for easy manipulation by patients
fields.name: Manual Controls

testCaseSteps.action: Take a "new patient" and measure how long it takes them to attach the hearing aid
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: patient is able to attach device in less than 10 seconds
testCaseSteps.notes: A new patient is one that's has not been released from the hospital after surgery

fields.description: Patients should be able to brush their teeth with the hearing aid connected
fields.name: Oral Hygien

testCaseSteps.action: Distribute the hearing aid to 10 patients
Have the patients brush their teeth with the hearing aid connected
testCaseSteps.expectedResult: All patients are able to brush their teeth without any hestitation

fields.description: Many hearing aids on the market require a pendant or other device to transfer sound between the hearing aid and the Android device. If we want to increase market share amongst younger patients, especially those who are Google-focused, we need to find a way to directly integrate with Android.
fields.name: Android Integration

fields.description: Many hearing aid on the market require a pendant or other device to transfer sound between the hearing aid and the iOS device. If we want to increase market share amongst younger patients, especially those who are Apple-focused, we need to find a way to directly integrate with iOS.
fields.name: iOS Integration

fields.description: Many hearing aid on the market require a pendant or other device to transfer sound between the hearing aid and the SymbianOS. If we want to increase market share amongst younger patients in Asia, primarily Japan, we need to find a way to directly integrate with SymbianOS.
fields.name: SymbianOS Integration

fields.description: Many hearing aids on the market require a pendant or other device to transfer sound between the hearing aid and the Windows device. If we want to increase market share amongst younger patients, especially those who are Windows-focused, we need to find a way to directly integrate with Windows.
fields.name: Windows Integration

fields.description:
A waterproof device is vital to increasing our market share. There's a large market, especially in the United States, that could be taken over through creation of a waterproof device.

 

This also would increase our ability to attract and retain retain younger customers, and may affect our ability to retain older as well.

 

Currently, processor specifically is not waterproof, so it has to be removed or covered with a special cover prior to showering or swimming.
fields.name: Waterproof

fields.description:
Hearing aids can be durable and stocky, or they can be small, thin, and invisible. In order to gain market share with those 35 and under, we need a hearing aid to reflect the active lifestyle.

 

Durability covers a wide arena of activities. Some examples:

 

  • During contact sports now, the device must be removed, with the abutment covered.
  • At the hairdresser, extra caution must be exercised.
  • Various weather conditions can negatively affect it (wind [dust], humidity, etc.).
fields.name: Durable

fields.description: There are times when our patients will turn off their hearing needs in order to get silence, or be able to concentrate more. During these times, or during times of other loud noises, there should be a way for our patients to recognize that there is a need for them to pay attention and turn on their hearing device. By providing haptic feedback, we can alert them in a not overly-distracting way.
fields.name: Haptic Feedback

fields.description: To minimize impact to the patient's life, surgery shall be single stage.
fields.name: Surgical Installation

fields.description: Average hearing gain in a free field pure tone audiogram should be in 31 +/- 10 dB.
fields.name: Hearing Gain in Free Field Pure Tone Audiogram

fields.description: The suprathreshold speech perception at 65 dB in free field pure tone audiogram should increase to 65-85%.
fields.name: Suprathreshold Speech Perception

Sound processor meets minimum requirements for release

fields.description: A detachable electronic hearing aid with a snap-fit coupling to the abutment. The user takes the sound processor on and off as required, for example for hair washing or swimming
fields.name: Sound Processor

fields.description: The battery door shall be tamper resistant.
fields.name: Battery Door

fields.description: The sound processor shall have a volume rocker to control volume.
fields.name: Volume Control

fields.description: The sound processor shall be IP57 classified.
fields.name: Environmental Resistance

fields.description:
Design based off of previous version of clEAR. Would like slightly more rounded and smaller size (1cm x 0.5 cm x 0.25 cm). Should be as unobtrusive as possible.

 

 

Given this is for the active lifecycle, it should be produced using a durable yet inexpensive plastic compound.
fields.name: Sound Processor

fields.description: Can be a simple medical-grade titanium with appropriate medically-approved coating. Sizing for each is being discussed and approved by our SMEs.
fields.name: Materials for Abutment and Implant

fields.description: The external abutment shall be made of appropriate materials per market research and specification.
fields.name: External Abutment

fields.description: The implant should be made of market and research-approved materials to specification.
fields.name: Implant
Dime in place of clEAR screwdriver

testCaseSteps.action: Using a dime, attempt to unscrew the battery door. If it cannot be done, verification passes.

fields.name: Paperclip in place of clEAR screwdriver

testCaseSteps.action: Using a paperclip, attempt to unscrew the battery door. If it cannot be done, verification passes.

fields.name: Standard-sized screwdriver in place of clEAR screwdriver

testCaseSteps.action: Using a standard-sized screwdriver, attempt to unscrew the battery door. If it cannot be done, verification passes.
When volume rocker is in off position, no db registers

fields.name: Volume rocker on and at minimum

testCaseSteps.action: Measure gain at low / minimum level and ensure it is within limits as dictated by compliance requirements.

Measure gain at medium level and ensure it is within limits as dictated by compliance requirements.

fields.description:
Introduction

The hearing aid has two major components:  a Detachable Processor Unit (DPU) and an abutment.  The abutment is surgically installed and used to conduct acoustic energy through the bone directly to the inner ear.  The DPU is used to:

 

  1. Pick up sound in the air and couple it onto the abutment
  2. Connect to electronic devices (eg cell phones)
  3. Provide haptic feedback to the patient

 

The connection to electronic devices will be achieved using Bluetooth technology and/or Wi-Fi.  The software requirements for the DPU will focus mainly on:

  • driving Piezo-Electric materials that couple energy onto the abutment
  • formatting messages for transmission over Wi-Fi
  • formatting messages for transmission over Bluetooth.  The BT stack is shown below
fields.name: Introduction

fields.description: The DPU shall automatically adjust gain control based on the patient base profile and the ambient noise.
fields.name: Automatic Gain Control

fields.description: The DPU shall check for software updates nightly
fields.name: Refresh Rate

fields.description: The DPU shall be capable of alerting the patient when there is noise above a pre-defined threshold
fields.name: Sound Detection

fields.description: The DPU shall generate vibrations at 1 KHz for patient alerts at the "critical level"
fields.name: Alert Vibration

fields.description: The DPU shall emit vibrations at 500 MHz for alerts at the "notification" level
fields.name: Notification Vibration

fields.description: The software shall be able to format messages appropriate for Bluetooth Basic Rate and Enhanced Data Rate (BR/EDR)
fields.name: Communication Rate

fields.description: The software shall be able to format messages appropriate for Bluetooth Low Energy
fields.name: Low Energy Communication

fields.description: A small titanium screw, four millimeters long, implanted into the bone behind the ear. The fixture is permanent, it is not adjusted or removed. The metal becomes firmly anchored to living bone by the process of osseo-integration.
fields.name: Implant

fields.description: The titanium implant shall have a diameter of ∅ 4.25mm.
fields.name: Diameter

fields.description: The implant shall have an ISQ between 54 and 61.
fields.name: Implant Stability Quotient (ISQ) Range

fields.description: The interface shall be backward compatible.
fields.name: Interface Requirements

fields.description: The implant shall have a length between 3 and 3.2mm.
fields.name: Length

fields.description: The implant shall be made of Grades 1-4 titanium per the ASTM-B265, ASTM-F67, and ASTMF-136 standards.
fields.name: Implant Material

fields.description: Take a random sample of five implants from manufacturer delivery.
fields.name: Verification of diameter

fields.description: A socket attached by an internal screw to the fixture. The abutment penetrates the surface of the scalp and is shaped to hold the snap-fit coupling of the sound processor. The abutment can be unscrewed from the fixture for maintenance or replacement by the specialist audiologist.
fields.name: External Abutment

fields.description: Upon surgical installation, the abutment shoulder should be placed above the skin.
fields.name: Abutment Shoulder

fields.description: Upon surgical installation, the skin will be flush all along the abutment interface.
fields.name: Skin Contact

fields.description: The abutment shall allow for less than 5% movement between the skin and the interface.
fields.name: Skin Movement

fields.description: The abutment shall support skin thicknesses between 2 and 2mm.
fields.name: Skin Thickness

fields.description: The abutment shall be adjustable by up to 8 degrees to allow for uniqueness in shape among patiences.
fields.name: Adjustable Angle

fields.description: The implant shall be made of Grades 1-4 titanium per the ASTM-B265, ASTM-F67, and ASTMF-136 standards.
fields.name: Abutment Material

fields.description: The abutment shall be coated with a hydroxyapatite [Ca10(PO4)6(OH)2] layer.
fields.name: Abutment Coating

fields.description: Infection, which can lead to death.
fields.detection_method$90: Observation of the implant site. Testing of blood.
fields.name: Bacterial infection
fields.rationale$90: During or after surgery, the patient can be exposed to microorganisms through contact with the air, infected caregivers, surgical instruments, or the device itself. We are primarily concerned with exposure because of the device itself.

fields.description: When osseointegraton fails, the implant must be removed and cannot be re-implanted for 9-12 months.
fields.detection_method$90: Body's rejection of the foreign matter by outgrowth.
fields.name: Implant doesn't integrate with bone because of patient movement
fields.rationale$90: Patient movement (sleeping, general movements throughout the day) could affect the implant.

fields.description: When osseointegraton fails, the implant must be removed and cannot be re-implanted for 9-12 months.
fields.detection_method$90: Body's rejection of the foreign matter by outgrowth.
fields.name: Implant doesn't integrate with bone because of external impacts

fields.description: Open wound leading to illness or death.
fields.name: Loss of osseointegrated fixture
